zh.lifeSummary: 當Payne de Beauchamp於大約1109年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾賀福德時，他的父親Robert De Beauchamp30歲，母親Isabel Neirunt30歲。他於大約1146年與Rohese de Vere在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1156年在英國貝德福爾貝德福德享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭貝德福爾貝德福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Payne de Beauchamp was born about 1109, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert De Beauchamp, was 30 and his mother, Isabel Neirunt, was 30. He married Rohese de Vere about 1146, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died about 1156, in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 49, and was buried in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Payne de Beauchamp est né environ 1109, à Hereford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert De Beauchamp, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Isabel Neirunt, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Rohese de Vere environ 1146, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé environ 1156, à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat um 1146 in Essex, England, United Kingdom Rohese de Vere geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. ja.lifeSummary: Payne de Beauchampが約1109年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアハーエフォードで生まれた時，父Robert De Beauchampは30歳，母Isabel Neiruntは30歳であった。約1146年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでRohese de Vereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は約1156年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアベドフォードで49歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアベドフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Payne de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1109년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert De Beauchamp(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Neirunt(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1146년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Rohese de Vere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1156년 영국 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Payne de Beauchamp родился около 1109 г. в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert De Beauchamp было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Neirunt, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Rohese de Vere около 1146 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер около 1156 г. в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
